purpose: End-to-end accessioning automation platform (IAW) for NeoGenomics, centered on a deterministic Validation & Decision Framework (VDF) rule engine. Framework decides; application acts.

goals.title: Production enterprise-grade quality
goals.description: Every feature must meet enterprise production standards: Clean Architecture boundaries, full typing, structured logging, graceful error handling, input validation, idempotency where applicable, observability, and documented public APIs. No prototypes-as-final.

goals.title: Security & vulnerability verification
goals.description: Every feature must be security-reviewed and vulnerability-checked before its passes flag flips true. Includes: authn/authz (RBAC) on every endpoint, input/output validation, parameterized data access (no SQL injection), secrets never hard-coded, OWASP Top 10 review, dependency vulnerability scan (dotnet list package --vulnerable, npm audit), PHI/HIPAA data-handling controls (encryption at rest/in transit, audit, least privilege, no PHI in logs), and SAST. Security findings block closure.
verification: secrets via config/Key Vault stub only

goals.title: Rich UI/UX design quality
goals.description: All user-facing screens must be designed for a polished, professional clinical-operations experience — not plain scaffolds. Use the stitch MCP (create_design_system, generate_screen_from_text, edit_screens, fetch_screen_code) and/or the frontend-design skill to produce the design system and screen designs, then implement in React + Fluent UI v9. Maintain a consistent design system (tokens, components), accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA), responsive layout, and information density appropriate for accessioner worklists/incident workspaces.
verification: consistent Fluent UI v9 component usage

conventions.principle: No business rules hard-coded in services. All validation/derivation/routing/hold logic lives in the versioned VDF rule repository. Runtime is deterministic; NL authoring is compile-time only.

features.description: MVP-0 Foundation: .NET 8 Clean Architecture solution (Api, Application, Domain, Vdf, Infrastructure, Integrations), PostgreSQL + EF Core schema & migrations, Docker Compose, auth/RBAC baseline, React+Vite+Fluent UI shell, shared libs (logging, error handling, audit, outbox), seed/mock data generator.
acceptance: dotnet build succeeds for all projects
EF Core migrations apply to Postgres
React dev server boots and renders shell
seed generator populates meaningful mock data

features.description: MVP-1 VDF Decision Core: fact assembly, versioned/effective-dated rule repository, trigger engine, rule selector + dependency ordering, decision evaluator (WHEN/DECISION/ON SUCCESS/ON FAILURE), outcome dispatcher (5 groups), reconciler, explainability/decision-trace, rule schema + closed vocabulary catalog.
acceptance: the 10 reference rules from the Translation Reference evaluate correctly
every outcome carries a full decision trace

features.description: MVP-2 End-to-end Vertical Slice: mock NeoLINK intake -> Order Worklist -> accession -> VDF evaluates -> Complete/Partial Hold -> Incident workspace -> resolve -> release-from-hold -> reconciler closes. Wires thin Core Domain + Worklist + Accessioning + PM + rules PM17/PM48/BL3/BL27.
features.notes: Proves the architecture end to end.

features.description: MVP-3 Core Domain & Order Worklist (full): Order/Test/Specimen/Patient/Client/Provider services + APIs, workflow/lifecycle engine, audit log, unified worklist (filter/sort/search/realtime/RBAC), priority (STAT>RUSH>Ped>Routine) + 90-day aging, Order Architecture UI (multi-test/specimen, draft, override). BRD-02, BRD-03.
acceptance: all domain entities CRUD + lifecycle transitions

features.description: MVP-4 Accessioning, Order Entry & Specimen Receipt: release online/interfaced + auto-submit, draft/activate, NPI validation + TCP routing, specimen receipt verification, patient resolution/dedup, duplicate-test detection, Interop hybrid reconciliation. BRD-03/04/05.

MVP-5 Problem Management & Action (full): incident & hold engine (all 30+ PM scenarios), hold queue + prioritization UI, incident workspace, notification & escalation framework, routing (review/medical/sendout), client resolution workflow + audit. BRD-07.

MVP-6 Business Logic rule corpus (full): author + test ~130 VDF rules (BL1-52, PM1-49) as configuration via the rule repository, including extension-point cases (time triggers, cross-entity, multi-record) and the 8 documented exceptions. BRD-08.
acceptance: all in-scope BL/PM rules authored as config
rule regression harness covers each rule with fact fixtures

features.description: MVP-7 Intake & Ingestion (full): mock NeoLINK (online OO1.0/2.0 + interfaced), OCR/IDP pipeline mock (confidence/draft), manual TRF screen, Patient Master mock, intake normalization + TC code translation. BRD-01.

MVP-8 Outbound Integrations & Sync: mock Salesforce bi-directional (PM events out, add-on/resolution events in via async callbacks), NeoLIMS specimen events (subscribe), Billing/PA workflow mock, downstream notification framework. BRD-10.
acceptance: PM events publish to mock SF
async resolution callbacks apply to incidents

features.description: MVP-9 Add-Ons, Reflex & Predictive: add-on ingestion (client + pathologist), add-on worklist + feasibility (Eligible/NotEligible/Review), reflex shells + auto-activate/close, predictive add-on recommendations + Pending Approval gate. BRD-09.
acceptance: add-on requests ingested + routed by specimen location

features.description: MVP-10 Rule Authoring NL Copilot & Governance: NL interpreter (constrained NL->structured rule, schema-bound), vocabulary linter + round-trip paraphrase + dry-run previewer, authoring UI + governance (review/approve/effective-date/promote), provenance store. BRD-11.
acceptance: NL input compiles to structured rule using only vocabulary terms
features.notes: NL is authoring-time only; compiled rule is source of truth.

features.description: MVP-11 Leadership Analytics & Dashboards: hold metrics (type/category/status/aging), resolution-time, throughput/bottlenecks, trend & anomaly, leadership dashboard with filters. BRD-12.

MVP-12 Quality, Security & Hardening: RBAC hardening + SSO stub, performance/load pass vs SLA targets, security review, full regression + E2E suite, docs (API contracts, runbooks, user guide). BRD-13.
acceptance: SLA targets met under load test
